ogTitle: Maloneys Beach vs Manar: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Maloneys Beach and Manar.
heading: Maloneys Beach vs Manar — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Maloneys Beach, New South Wales and Manar, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Maloneys Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Manar (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Maloneys Beach or Manar safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Maloneys Beach has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Manar scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Maloneys Beach scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Manar scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Maloneys Beach or Manar?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Maloneys Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.28% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
